articles.title: UNC cancer center awarded $28M to lead ‘adaptive’ clinical trial
articles.description: Chapel Hill-based University of North Carolina Lineberger Comprehensive Cancer Center has been awarded up to $28 million to develop an adaptive clinical trial targeting metastatic breast cancer. The trial is part of the Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health’s Advanced Analysis for Precision Cancer Therapy program, also called ADAPT. Other ADAPT awardees and their trial […] The post UNC cancer center awarded $28M to lead ‘adaptive’ clinical trial appeared first on Becker's Hospital Review | Healthcare News & Analysis .
articles.author: Elizabeth Gregerson

articles.title: NIH researchers develop AI agent that improves accuracy of gene set analysis by leveraging expert-curated databases
articles.description: The AI agent could help lead to a better understanding of how different diseases and conditions affect groups of genes individually and together.

articles.title: Can an AI chatbot of Dr Karl change climate sceptics’ minds? He’s willing to give it a try
articles.description: The famous science communicator is planning to release a chatbot designed to answer questions on the climate crisis. Can it work? There’s arguably no face, voice or collection of exuberant, patterned shirts more recognisable than those belonging to Dr Karl Kruszelnicki. The bespectacled boffin has been answering curly listener questions about science, with characteristic excitement and passion, for more than 40 years. Despite a seemingly tireless work ethic, Kruszelnicki, now 77 years old, can’t be everywhere all at once. Continue reading...
articles.author: Jackson Ryan

articles.title: NASA and Google developed an AI medical assistant to keep astronauts healthy on deep-space missions
articles.description: To address this challenge, NASA is partnering with Google to develop an artificial intelligence tool that could help astronauts diagnose and treat illnesses without real-time support from Earth. The system, called the Crew Medical Officer Digital Assistant (CMO-DA), is designed to guide medical decision-making in environments where communication is slow... Read Entire Article
articles.author: Skye Jacobs

articles.title: Artificial intelligence is revolutionizing medical image analysis
articles.description: One in two Australians regularly use artificial intelligence (AI), with that number expected to grow. AI is showing up in our lives more prominently than ever, with the arrival of ChatGPT and other chatbots.
articles.image_path: src/frontend/assets/images/articles/2025-08-artificial-intelligence-revolutionizing-medical-image.html_Artificial intelligence is revolutionizing medical image analysis

articles.title: Man Follows ChatGPT's Advice and Poisons Himself
articles.description: A man trying to cut out salt from his diet learned the hard way that ChatGPT isn't to be trusted with medical advice after the OpenAI chatbot's toxic suggestions landed him in the hospital. As described in a new paper published in the Annals of Internal Medicine journal, a 60-year-old man ended up coming down with an all-but-defunct psychiatric condition known as "bromism" after ChatGPT suggested he replace sodium chloride, which is better known as table salt, with sodium bromide, a substance used in pesticides, pool and hot tub cleaners, and as a canine anticonvulsant. Though his doctors never got […]
